data.title: NewAmsterdam Pharma: Advancing EU Launch and CV Outcomes Pathway Underpins Unchanged $48 Price Target and Buy Rating
data.description:
Needham analyst Serge Belanger has maintained their bullish stance on NAMS stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Serge Belanger has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to obicetrapib’s advancing regulatory and commercial profile. The positive CHMP opinion for Ubeslo and Evlarco positions NewAmsterdam Pharma for its first regulatory approval in the EU, enabling Menarini to launch the products across European markets and triggering tiered double-digit royalty income for NAMS.

Belanger also highlights the importance of upcoming clinical and investor catalysts that can further validate obicetrapib’s cardiovascular benefits and commercial potential. The planned interim analysis of the PREVAIL CVOT trial, expected in late 2026 with data in early 2027, is central to future labeling, guideline inclusion, and broader uptake, supporting his unchanged $48 price target and Buy recommendation on NAMS.

In another report released today, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $52.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Deckers: Strong Core Brand Momentum and Raised EPS Outlook Support Buy Rating; $160 Price Target Reiterated
data.description:
Williams Trading analyst Sam Poser has maintained their bullish stance on DECK stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Sam Poser has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that emphasize the underlying strength of Deckers’ core brands despite near‑term noise around guidance. He highlights that demand for both HOKA and UGG remains robust, as shown by solid direct‑to‑consumer growth, and notes that weaker wholesale trends in Europe largely stem from last year’s early shipments rather than any deterioration in consumer interest.

Poser also underscores that management raised full‑year EPS guidance, which now sits slightly above consensus at the high end, and he points to Deckers’ consistent record of outperforming expectations. Although second‑quarter guidance appears conservative given higher tariffs, freight, and elevated SG&A from strategic investments, he believes these costs support long‑term brand momentum and should drive upside in the back half of the year, justifying a Buy rating and an unchanged $160 price target.

Poser covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Deckers Outdoor, Steven Madden, and Crocs. According to TipRanks, Poser has an average return of 17.8% and a 52.54% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $133.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Neal Dingmann Reiterates Buy on Crescent, Citing Record FCF Potential, Permian Efficiency Gains, and 23% Upside Fair Value
data.description:
William Blair analyst Neal Dingmann has maintained their bullish stance on CRGY stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Neal Dingmann has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to strong free cash flow generation and disciplined capital management. He expects Crescent to deliver potentially record second-quarter free cash flow, supported by resilient production, favorable commodity pricing, ongoing cost reductions, and meaningful synergies from recently integrated Vital assets, while maintaining a moderate spending profile.

He also highlights improving operational efficiency across the portfolio, particularly in the Permian, where optimized drilling and completion performance and lower well costs enhance returns even as activity levels normalize. With additional upside from prospective bolt-on acquisitions, updated synergy targets, and a fair value estimate implying about 23% price appreciation using conservative long-term oil and gas assumptions, he views the stock’s risk/reward as attractive, justifying his Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Dingmann is an analyst with an average return of -3.2% and a 39.83% success rate. Dingmann covers the Energy sector, focusing on stocks such as Matador Resources, Ovintiv, and Northern Oil And Gas.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $18.00 price target.
